transaction:
    - name: trn_voucher
      collection: Voucher
      nature: Primary
      fetch:
        - Narration,PartyLedgerName,PlaceOfSupply,Reference,ReferenceDate
      filters:
        - NOT $IsCancelled
        - NOT $IsOptional
      fields:
        - name: guid
          field: Guid
          type: text
        - name: date
          field: Date
          type: date
        - name: voucher_type
          field: VoucherTypeName
          type: text
        - name: voucher_number
          field: VoucherNumber
          type: text
        - name: reference_number
          field: Reference
          type: text

          field: ReferenceDate
          type: date

          field: Narration
          type: text
        - name: party_name
          field: PartyLedgerName
          type: text
        - name: place_of_supply
          field: PlaceOfSupply
          type: text
        - name: is_invoice
          field: IsInvoice
          type: logical
        - name: is_accounting_voucher
          field: if $$IsAccountingVch:$VoucherTypeName then 1 else 0
          type: logical
        - name: is_inventory_voucher
          field: if $$IsInventoryVch:$VoucherTypeName then 1 else 0
          type: logical
        - name: is_order_voucher
          field: if $$IsOrderVch:$VoucherTypeName then 1 else 0
          type: logical
    - name: trn_accounting
      collection: Voucher.AllLedgerEntries
      nature: Derived
      fetch:
        - AllLedgerEntries
      filters:
        - NOT $IsCancelled
        - NOT $IsOptional
        - $$NumItems:AllLedgerEntries > 0
      fields:
        - name: guid
          field: Guid
          type: text
        - name: ledger
          field: LedgerName
          type: text
        - name: amount
          field: Amount
          type: amount
        - name: amount_forex
          field: if $$IsEmpty:$$ForexValue:$Amount then 0 else $$StringFindAndReplace:(if $$IsDebit:$Amount then -$$ForexValue:$Amount else $$ForexValue:$Amount):"(-)":"-"
          type: amount
        - name: currency
          field: $$Currency:$Amount
          type: text
    - name: trn_inventory

          type: amount
        - name: discount_amount

          type: number

        - name: tracking_number
          field: if ($$IsEmpty:$TrackingNumber or $$IsNotApplicable:$TrackingNumber) then "" else $TrackingNumber
          type: text
        - name: order_number
          field: if ($$IsEmpty:$OrderNo or $$IsNotApplicable:$OrderNo) then "" else $OrderNo
          type: text
        - name: order_duedate
          field: OrderDueDate
          type: date

# TallySyncService - Complete Setup (Final Version)
# This script publishes, copies config files, and sets up auto-start

# Check if running as Administrator
$currentPrincipal = New-Object Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal([Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent())
$isAdmin = $currentPrincipal.IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]::Administrator)

if (-not $isAdmin) {
    Write-Host "This script needs Administrator privileges to manage scheduled tasks." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Write-Host "Restarting as Administrator..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Write-Host ""
    
    # Restart as admin
    Start-Process powershell.exe -Verb RunAs -ArgumentList "-NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "$($MyInvocation.MyCommand.Path)""
    exit
}

Write-Host "===============================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  TallySyncService - Complete Setup" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "  Running as Administrator" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "===============================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

$TaskName = "TallySyncService"
$ProjectDir = Split-Path -Parent $MyInvocation.MyCommand.Path

# STEP 1: Clean up everything first
Write-Host "STEP 1: Cleaning up old installations..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "========================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

# Remove old scheduled task
Write-Host "Removing old scheduled task..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
$ExistingTask = Get-ScheduledTask -TaskName $TaskName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if ($ExistingTask) {
    Stop-ScheduledTask -TaskName $TaskName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    Start-Sleep -Seconds 2
    Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName $TaskName -Confirm:$false -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    Write-Host "[OK] Old task removed" -ForegroundColor Green
} else {
    Write-Host "[OK] No existing task found" -ForegroundColor Green
}

# Kill any running processes
Write-Host "Stopping any running service instances..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
Get-Process | Where-Object {
    $_.ProcessName -like "TallySync" -or
    ($.Path -and $.Path -like "TallySyncService")
} | Stop-Process -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
Write-Host "[OK] Processes stopped" -ForegroundColor Green

# Remove old launcher files
Write-Host "Removing old launcher files..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
@("$ProjectDir\run-hidden.vbs", "$ProjectDir\start-service-hidden.ps1") | ForEach-Object {
    if (Test-Path $) { Remove-Item $ -Force }
}
Write-Host "[OK] Old files cleaned up" -ForegroundColor Green

Write-Host ""

# STEP 2: Check for .NET SDK
Write-Host "STEP 2: Checking prerequisites..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "===================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

$DotnetPath = (Get-Command dotnet -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Source
if (-not $DotnetPath) {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] dotnet command not found!" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "You need .NET SDK installed to publish the application." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Read-Host "Press Enter to exit"
    exit 1
}
Write-Host "[OK] .NET SDK found: $DotnetPath" -ForegroundColor Green

# Find project file
$ProjectFile = Get-ChildItem -Path $ProjectDir -Filter "*.csproj" | Select-Object -First 1
if (-not $ProjectFile) {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] No .csproj file found!" -ForegroundColor Red
    Read-Host "Press Enter to exit"
    exit 1
}
Write-Host "[OK] Project file: $($ProjectFile.Name)" -ForegroundColor Green

Write-Host ""

# STEP 3: Publish the application
Write-Host "STEP 3: Publishing self-contained executable..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "================================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "Creating standalone .exe (no .NET needed on customer machines)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host "Please wait..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host ""

$PublishDir = "$ProjectDir\publish"

# Remove old publish folder
if (Test-Path $PublishDir) {
    Write-Host "Removing old publish folder..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Remove-Item -Path $PublishDir -Recurse -Force
}

# Publish command
$PublishArgs = @(
    "publish"
    ""$($ProjectFile.FullName)""
    "-c", "Release"
    "-r", "win-x64"
    "--self-contained", "true"
    "-p:PublishSingleFile=true"
    "-p:IncludeNativeLibrariesForSelfExtract=true"
    "-p:PublishTrimmed=false"
    "-o", ""$PublishDir""
)

& $DotnetPath $PublishArgs | Out-Null

if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] Failed to publish!" -ForegroundColor Red
    Read-Host "Press Enter to exit"
    exit 1
}

Write-Host "[SUCCESS] Application published!" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host ""

# STEP 4: Copy configuration files
Write-Host "STEP 4: Copying configuration files..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "=======================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

# Copy all necessary config files to publish folder
$ConfigFiles = @(
    "tally-export-config.yaml",
    "appsettings.json",
    "appsettings.Development.json",
    "appsettings.Production.json"
)

foreach ($configFile in $ConfigFiles) {
    $sourcePath = "$ProjectDir\$configFile"
    if (Test-Path $sourcePath) {
        Copy-Item -Path $sourcePath -Destination $PublishDir -Force
        Write-Host "[OK] Copied: $configFile" -ForegroundColor Green
    } else {
        Write-Host "[SKIP] Not found: $configFile" -ForegroundColor Gray
    }
}

Write-Host ""

# STEP 5: Verify executable
Write-Host "STEP 5: Verifying published files..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "=====================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

$ExeFile = Get-ChildItem -Path $PublishDir -Filter "*.exe" | Select-Object -First 1
if (-not $ExeFile) {
    Write-Host "[ERROR] No .exe file found in publish directory!" -ForegroundColor Red
    Read-Host "Press Enter to exit"
    exit 1
}

$ExePath = $ExeFile.FullName
Write-Host "[OK] Executable: $($ExeFile.Name)" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "     Size: $([math]::Round($ExeFile.Length / 1MB, 2)) MB" -ForegroundColor Gray
Write-Host "     Path: $ExePath" -ForegroundColor Gray

Write-Host ""

# STEP 6: Create hidden launcher
Write-Host "STEP 6: Creating hidden launcher..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "====================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

$VbsPath = "$PublishDir\run-hidden.vbs"
$VbsContent = @"
Set WshShell = CreateObject("WScript.Shell")
WshShell.Run """$ExePath""", 0, False
Set WshShell = Nothing
"@
$VbsContent | Out-File -FilePath $VbsPath -Encoding ASCII -Force
Write-Host "[OK] Launcher created" -ForegroundColor Green

Write-Host ""

# STEP 7: Create scheduled task
Write-Host "STEP 7: Creating scheduled task..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "===================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

# Double-check no task exists
$CheckTask = Get-ScheduledTask -TaskName $TaskName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
if ($CheckTask) {
    Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName $TaskName -Confirm:$false -ErrorAction Stop
    Start-Sleep -Seconds 2
}

# Create the action
$Action = New-ScheduledTaskAction `
    -Execute "wscript.exe" `
    -Argument ""$VbsPath"" `
    -WorkingDirectory "$PublishDir"

# Create the trigger (at user login)
$Trigger = New-ScheduledTaskTrigger -AtLogOn -User $env:USERNAME

# Create settings
$Settings = New-ScheduledTaskSettingsSet `
    -AllowStartIfOnBatteries `
    -DontStopIfGoingOnBatteries `
    -StartWhenAvailable `
    -ExecutionTimeLimit (New-TimeSpan -Hours 0) `
    -RestartCount 3 `
    -RestartInterval (New-TimeSpan -Minutes 1)

# Create the principal
$Principal = New-ScheduledTaskPrincipal `
    -UserId $env:USERNAME `
    -LogonType Interactive `
    -RunLevel Limited

# Register the task
Register-ScheduledTask `
    -TaskName $TaskName `
    -Action $Action `
    -Trigger $Trigger `
    -Settings $Settings `
    -Principal $Principal `
    -Description "TallySyncService - Auto-sync Tally data (standalone, no .NET required)" -ErrorAction Stop | Out-Null

Write-Host "[OK] Scheduled task created" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host ""

# STEP 8: Start the service
Write-Host "STEP 8: Starting the service..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

Start-ScheduledTask -TaskName $TaskName
Write-Host "Waiting for service to start..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
Start-Sleep -Seconds 5

# Check if it's running
$ExeName = $ExeFile.BaseName
$ServiceProcess = Get-Process -Name $ExeName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

Write-Host ""
if ($ServiceProcess) {
    Write-Host "===============================================" -ForegroundColor Green
    Write-Host "[SUCCESS] Service is running!" -ForegroundColor Green
    Write-Host "===============================================" -ForegroundColor Green
    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "Process Details:" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    Write-Host "  Name: $ExeName" -ForegroundColor White
    Write-Host "  PID:  $($ServiceProcess.Id)" -ForegroundColor White
    Write-Host "  Path: $ExePath" -ForegroundColor White
    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "The service is now running HIDDEN in the background!" -ForegroundColor Cyan
    Write-Host "It will sync every 15 minutes and auto-start on login." -ForegroundColor Cyan
} else {
    Write-Host "[WARNING] Service process not found in Task Manager" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Write-Host "This might be normal - the service may have started and is waiting." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "To verify, check Task Manager for '$ExeName.exe'" -ForegroundColor Cyan
}

Write-Host ""
Write-Host "===============================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "Setup Complete!" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "===============================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "Installation Summary:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host "  Location:      $PublishDir" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Executable:    TallySyncService.exe" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Task Name:     $TaskName" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Auto-start:    Enabled (on login)" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Runs Hidden:   Yes (no console)" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Needs .NET:    No (self-contained)" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "For Customer Deployment:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host "  1. Copy the entire 'publish' folder to customer machine" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  2. Make sure config files are in the publish folder" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  3. Run this script as Administrator" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  4. No .NET installation needed!" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "Management Commands:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host "  Start:    Start-ScheduledTask -TaskName $TaskName" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Stop:     Stop-ScheduledTask -TaskName $TaskName" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Status:   Get-Process $ExeName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "  Remove:   Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName $TaskName" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host ""

Read-Host "Press Enter to close"

# TallySyncService - Customer Setup Guide

## 📋 Overview

**TallySyncService** is a background service that automatically synchronizes data from TallyPrime to your backend system. It runs continuously in the background, exporting data from Tally at regular intervals and uploading it to your cloud backend.

### Key Features

- ✅ Automatic data synchronization from TallyPrime
- ✅ Configurable sync intervals
- ✅ Support for Master and Transaction tables
- ✅ Selective table export (choose specific tables)
- ✅ Secure authentication with OTP-based login
- ✅ Cross-platform support (Windows & Linux)
- ✅ Runs as a background service

---

## 🔧 Prerequisites

Before setting up TallySyncService, ensure you have:

1. **TallyPrime** installed and running
   - Tally must be accessible on the network
   - XML interface must be enabled (enabled by default)
   - Default port: `9000`

2. **.NET 8.0 Runtime** or SDK installed
   - Download from: https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/8.0
   - For Windows: Install the .NET 8.0 Runtime
   - For Linux: Install the .NET 8.0 SDK

3. **Backend Access**
   - Backend URL (provided by your administrator)
   - Valid email account registered in the system
   - Organization access

---

## 📦 Installation

### Step 1: Download the Service

Download the TallySyncService package for your platform:

- **Windows**: `TallySyncService-win-x64.zip`
- **Linux**: `TallySyncService-linux-x64.tar.gz`

### Step 2: Extract Files

Extract the downloaded package to a location of your choice:

**Windows:**
```cmd
# Extract to C:\TallySyncService
```

**Linux:**
```bash
# Extract to /opt/TallySyncService
tar -xzf TallySyncService-linux-x64.tar.gz -C /opt/TallySyncService
```

### Step 3: Verify Installation

Navigate to the installation directory and verify the files:

**Windows:**
```cmd
cd C:\TallySyncService
dir
```

**Linux:**
```bash
cd /opt/TallySyncService
ls -la
```

You should see files including:
- `TallySyncService.exe` (Windows) or `TallySyncService` (Linux)
- `appsettings.json`
- `tally-export-config.yaml`
- `config.sample.json`

---

## ⚙️ Configuration

### Step 1: Run Setup Command

The setup wizard will guide you through the configuration process.

**Windows:**
```cmd
TallySyncService.exe --setup
```

**Linux:**
```bash
dotnet TallySyncService.dll --setup
```

### Step 2: Configure Settings

The setup wizard will prompt you for the following information:

#### 1. **Tally Configuration**
```
Tally Server [localhost]: localhost
Tally Port [9000]: 9000
Company Name (leave empty to select at runtime): 
```

- **Tally Server**: IP address or hostname where Tally is running
  - Use `localhost` if Tally is on the same machine
  - Use IP address (e.g., `192.168.1.100`) if Tally is on another machine
- **Tally Port**: Default is `9000`
- **Company Name**: Leave empty to select from available companies at runtime

#### 2. **Sync Configuration**
```
Sync Interval (minutes) [15]: 15
Export Path [./exports]: ./exports
```

- **Sync Interval**: How often to sync data (in minutes)
  - Recommended: `15` minutes
  - Minimum: `5` minutes
- **Export Path**: Where to temporarily store CSV files

#### 3. **Backend Configuration**
```
Backend Base URL (e.g., http://localhost:3001): https://your-backend-url.com
```

- Enter the backend URL provided by your administrator
- Example: `https://dhub-backend-dev.onlyoncloud.com`

#### 4. **Table Selection**
```
Which tables do you want to export?
  1. Master Tables only
  2. Transaction Tables only
  3. All Tables (Master + Transaction)
  4. Custom Selection
Select option [3]: 3
```

**Options:**
- **Option 1 (Master Tables)**: Exports only master data (Ledgers, Stock Items, etc.)
- **Option 2 (Transaction Tables)**: Exports only transaction data (Vouchers, Accounting, Inventory)
- **Option 3 (All Tables)**: Exports both master and transaction data (Recommended)
- **Option 4 (Custom Selection)**: Choose specific tables to export

### Step 3: Verify Configuration

After setup completes, the wizard will:
- Save configuration to `config.json`
- Test connection to Tally
- Display available companies

Example output:
```
✅ Configuration saved to config.json
Testing Tally connection...
✓ Successfully connected to Tally at localhost:9000
✓ Found 2 company(ies):
  • Sample Company Ltd.
  • Test Company Pvt. Ltd.

Setup complete! You can now:
  1. Login: dotnet run -- --login https://your-backend-url.com
  2. Start sync: dotnet run
```

---

## 🔐 Authentication

### Step 1: Login to Backend

Before starting the sync service, you must authenticate with the backend.

**Windows:**
```cmd
TallySyncService.exe --login https://your-backend-url.com
```

**Linux:**
```bash
dotnet TallySyncService.dll --login https://your-backend-url.com
```

### Step 2: Enter Email

```
Enter your email: your.email@example.com
```

Enter the email address registered in the backend system.

### Step 3: Enter OTP

```
Sending OTP to your email...
✓ OTP sent successfully

Enter the OTP from your email: 123456
```

Check your email for the OTP code and enter it.

### Step 4: Select Organization

```
Validating OTP...
✓ OTP validated successfully

Fetching your organizations...

Found 2 organization(s):
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1. ACME Corp (ID: 101)
  2. XYZ Industries (ID: 102)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────

Select organization (1-2): 1
✓ Selected: ACME Corp

✅ Login successful!
   Token and organization saved.
   You can now use the sync service.
```

> **Note**: Your authentication token is saved in `~/.tally_token` and organization ID in `~/.tally_org`

---

## 🚀 Running the Service

### Manual Start (Testing)

To run the service manually for testing:

**Windows:**
```cmd
TallySyncService.exe
```

**Linux:**
```bash
dotnet TallySyncService.dll
```

You should see output like:
```
╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║   Tally CSV Sync Service (Background)         ║
║   Syncing every 15 minutes                    ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════╝

═══════════════════════════════════════════════
🔄 Starting sync at 2026-02-06 20:00:00
═══════════════════════════════════════════════
Testing connection to Tally...
✓ Connected to Tally
✓ Loaded 15 table(s) to export (mode: all)
✓ Using company: Sample Company Ltd.

📤 Exporting 15 tables...
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ✓ mst_ledger (1,234 records)
  ✓ mst_stock_item (567 records)
  ✓ trn_voucher (8,901 records)
  ...
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
✓ Exported 15 files

📡 Uploading to backend...
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ✓ Uploaded mst_ledger.csv
  ✓ Uploaded mst_stock_item.csv
  ...
─────────────────────────────────────────────────
✓ Uploaded 15/15 files successfully
✓ Cleaned up temporary files

✅ Sync completed successfully at 2026-02-06 20:05:23

⏱️  Next sync in 15 minutes...
```

### Running as a Service (Production)

For production use, configure the service to run automatically in the background.

#### Windows Service

1. **Install as Windows Service** (requires Administrator privileges):

```cmd
sc create TallySyncService binPath="C:\TallySyncService\TallySyncService.exe" start=auto
sc description TallySyncService "Tally Data Synchronization Service"
```

2. **Start the service:**

```cmd
sc start TallySyncService
```

3. **Check service status:**

```cmd
sc query TallySyncService
```

4. **Stop the service:**

```cmd
sc stop TallySyncService
```

5. **Remove the service** (if needed):

```cmd
sc delete TallySyncService
```

#### Linux systemd Service

1. **Create systemd service file:**

Edit the provided `tallysync.service` file:

```bash
sudo nano /etc/systemd/system/tallysync.service
```

Update the paths in the file:

```ini
[Unit]
Description=Tally Sync Service
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=your-username
WorkingDirectory=/opt/TallySyncService
ExecStart=/usr/bin/dotnet /opt/TallySyncService/TallySyncService.dll
Restart=always
RestartSec=10

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

2. **Enable and start the service:**

```bash
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable tallysync.service
sudo systemctl start tallysync.service
```

3. **Check service status:**

```bash
sudo systemctl status tallysync.service
```

4. **View logs:**

```bash
sudo journalctl -u tallysync.service -f
```

5. **Stop the service:**

```bash
sudo systemctl stop tallysync.service
```

---

## 📝 Configuration Reference

### config.json Structure

```json
{
  "tally": {
    "server": "localhost",
    "port": 9000,
    "company": "Sample Company Ltd."
  },
  "sync": {
    "intervalMinutes": 15,
    "exportPath": "./exports"
  },
  "backend": {
    "url": "https://your-backend-url.com/api/data"
  },
  "tables": {
    "mode": "all",
    "customTables": []
  }
}
```

### Configuration Options

| Section | Field | Description | Default |
|---------|-------|-------------|---------|
| **tally** | server | Tally server IP/hostname | `localhost` |
| | port | Tally XML port | `9000` |
| | company | Company name (empty = auto-select) | `""` |
| **sync** | intervalMinutes | Sync frequency in minutes | `15` |
| | exportPath | Temporary CSV export directory | `./exports` |
| **backend** | url | Backend API endpoint | - |
| **tables** | mode | Export mode: `all`, `master`, `transaction`, `custom` | `all` |
| | customTables | Array of table names (for `custom` mode) | `[]` |

### Table Modes

- **`all`**: Exports all master and transaction tables
- **`master`**: Exports only master tables (Ledgers, Stock Items, etc.)
- **`transaction`**: Exports only transaction tables (Vouchers, Accounting, etc.)
- **`custom`**: Exports only specified tables in `customTables` array

Example custom configuration:
```json
{
  "tables": {
    "mode": "custom",
    "customTables": [
      "mst_ledger",
      "mst_stock_item",
      "trn_voucher"
    ]
  }
}
```

---

## 🔍 Troubleshooting

### Issue: Cannot connect to Tally

**Symptoms:**
```
✗ Unable to connect to Tally at localhost:9000
```

**Solutions:**
1. Verify Tally is running
2. Check Tally port configuration (default: 9000)
3. Ensure XML interface is enabled in Tally
4. If Tally is on another machine, verify network connectivity
5. Check firewall settings

### Issue: No companies found

**Symptoms:**
```
✗ No companies found
```

**Solutions:**
1. Ensure at least one company is loaded in Tally
2. Verify Tally XML interface is responding
3. Check Tally license and permissions

### Issue: Login failed

**Symptoms:**
```
✗ Failed to send OTP. Please try again.
```

**Solutions:**
1. Verify backend URL is correct
2. Check internet connectivity
3. Ensure email is registered in the backend system
4. Contact your administrator for account access

### Issue: No organization selected

**Symptoms:**
```
✗ No organization selected. Please run: dotnet run -- --login
```

**Solutions:**
1. Run the login command: `TallySyncService.exe --login https://your-backend-url.com`
2. Complete the authentication process
3. Select an organization

### Issue: Upload failed

**Symptoms:**
```
✗ Uploaded 0/15 files successfully
```

**Solutions:**
1. Verify authentication token is valid (re-login if needed)
2. Check backend URL and connectivity
3. Ensure organization has proper permissions
4. Check backend logs for errors

### Issue: Service won't start (Windows)

**Solutions:**
1. Run Command Prompt as Administrator
2. Check service status: `sc query TallySyncService`
3. View Windows Event Viewer for error details
4. Verify .NET 8.0 Runtime is installed

### Issue: Service won't start (Linux)

**Solutions:**
1. Check service status: `sudo systemctl status tallysync.service`
2. View logs: `sudo journalctl -u tallysync.service -n 50`
3. Verify file permissions: `chmod +x TallySyncService.dll`
4. Ensure .NET 8.0 SDK is installed: `dotnet --version`

---

## 📊 Monitoring

### Check Sync Status

Monitor the service output to verify successful syncs:

**Windows:**
```cmd
# If running manually, check console output
# If running as service, check Windows Event Viewer
```

**Linux:**
```bash
# View real-time logs
sudo journalctl -u tallysync.service -f

# View last 100 lines
sudo journalctl -u tallysync.service -n 100
```

### Log Files

The service creates log files in the installation directory:
- `import-log.txt`: Successful operations
- `error-log.txt`: Error messages

### Successful Sync Indicators

Look for these messages:
- `✓ Connected to Tally`
- `✓ Loaded X table(s) to export`
- `✓ Exported X files`
- `✓ Uploaded X/X files successfully`
- `✅ Sync completed successfully`

---

## 🔄 Updating Configuration

To change configuration after initial setup:

1. **Stop the service** (if running as a service)

2. **Edit config.json** manually or run setup again:
   ```cmd
   TallySyncService.exe --setup
   ```

3. **Re-login** if backend URL changed:
   ```cmd
   TallySyncService.exe --login https://new-backend-url.com
   ```

4. **Restart the service**

---

## 📞 Support

If you encounter issues not covered in this guide:

1. Check the log files (`import-log.txt`, `error-log.txt`)
2. Verify all prerequisites are met
3. Contact your system administrator
4. Provide the following information:
   - Service version
   - Operating system
   - Error messages from logs
   - Configuration (without sensitive data)

---

## 📄 License

This software is proprietary. Unauthorized copying, distribution, or modification is prohibited.

---

## 🔖 Quick Reference

### Common Commands

| Action | Windows | Linux |
|--------|---------|-------|
| **Setup** | `TallySyncService.exe --setup` | `dotnet TallySyncService.dll --setup` |
| **Login** | `TallySyncService.exe --login <url>` | `dotnet TallySyncService.dll --login <url>` |
| **Run** | `TallySyncService.exe` | `dotnet TallySyncService.dll` |
| **Install Service** | `sc create TallySyncService ...` | `sudo systemctl enable tallysync.service` |
| **Start Service** | `sc start TallySyncService` | `sudo systemctl start tallysync.service` |
| **Stop Service** | `sc stop TallySyncService` | `sudo systemctl stop tallysync.service` |
| **Check Status** | `sc query TallySyncService` | `sudo systemctl status tallysync.service` |
| **View Logs** | Event Viewer | `sudo journalctl -u tallysync.service -f` |

---

**Last Updated:** February 2026

from urllib.parse import urlparse

# ===== CONFIG =====
INPUT_FILE = "input.txt"          # File containing text/HTML
OUTPUT_DIR = "downloads"          # Folder to save files
URL_PREFIX = r"https://help\.tallysolutions\.com/wp-content[^\s\"'>]+\.zip"
TIMEOUT = 30
# ==================

os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

def extract_urls(file_path):
    with open(file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as f:
        content = f.read()

    urls = re.findall(URL_PREFIX, content)
    return list(set(urls))  # remove duplicates


def download_file(url):
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url)
        filename = os.path.basename(parsed.path)

        if not filename:
            print(f"⚠️ Skipping (no filename): {url}")
            return

        output_path = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, filename)

        if os.path.exists(output_path):
            print(f"⏭️ Already exists: {filename}")
            return

        print(f"⬇️ Downloading: {url}")
        response = requests.get(url, timeout=TIMEOUT, stream=True)
        response.raise_for_status()

        with open(output_path, "wb") as f:
            for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=8192):
                if chunk:
                    f.write(chunk)

        print(f"✅ Saved: {filename}")

    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ Failed: {url} → {e}")


def main():
    urls = extract_urls(INPUT_FILE)
    for url in urls :download_file(url)
    print(f"🔎 Found {len(urls)} matching URLs")
 

    print("🎉 Download complete")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

# TallySyncService Auto-Start Setup Guide

This guide explains how to set up TallySyncService to automatically start when you log in to your computer.

## Prerequisites

Before setting up auto-start, make sure you have:

1. Completed the initial setup: `dotnet run -- --setup`
2. Logged in to the backend: `dotnet run -- --login <backend-url>`
3. Verified that the service works: `dotnet run`

## Linux Setup (systemd user service)

### Quick Setup

Run the setup script:

```bash
chmod +x setup-linux-autostart.sh
./setup-linux-autostart.sh
```

The script will:
- Create a systemd user service
- Enable it to start on login
- Start the service immediately
- Create log files in `~/.local/share/tallysync/`

### Manual Setup

If you prefer to set it up manually:

1. **Copy the service file:**
   ```bash
   mkdir -p ~/.config/systemd/user
   cp tallysync-user.service ~/.config/systemd/user/
   ```

2. **Edit the service file to update paths:**
   ```bash
   nano ~/.config/systemd/user/tallysync-user.service
   ```
   
   Update `WorkingDirectory` and `ExecStart` to match your project location.

3. **Enable and start the service:**
   ```bash
   systemctl --user daemon-reload
   systemctl --user enable tallysync-user.service
   systemctl --user start tallysync-user.service
   ```

### Managing the Linux Service

```bash
# Check status
systemctl --user status tallysync-user.service

# View live logs
journalctl --user -u tallysync-user.service -f

# Stop the service
systemctl --user stop tallysync-user.service

# Restart the service
systemctl --user restart tallysync-user.service

# Disable auto-start
systemctl --user disable tallysync-user.service

# View log files
tail -f ~/.local/share/tallysync/output.log
tail -f ~/.local/share/tallysync/error.log
```

---

## Windows Setup (Task Scheduler)

### Quick Setup

Run the setup script in PowerShell **as Administrator**:

```powershell
Set-ExecutionPolicy -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -Scope CurrentUser
.\setup-windows-autostart.ps1
```

The script will:
- Create a scheduled task named "TallySyncService"
- Configure it to start on user login
- Start the task immediately
- Create log directory in `%LOCALAPPDATA%\TallySyncService\Logs`

### Manual Setup

If you prefer to set it up manually:

1. **Open Task Scheduler:**
   - Press `Win + R`, type `taskschd.msc`, and press Enter

2. **Create a new task:**
   - Click "Create Task..." (not "Create Basic Task")
   - Name: `TallySyncService`
   - Description: `Automatically syncs Tally data to backend`
   - Select "Run whether user is logged on or not"
   - Check "Run with highest privileges"

3. **Configure Triggers:**
   - Click "Triggers" tab → "New..."
   - Begin the task: "At log on"
   - Specific user: (your username)
   - Click OK

4. **Configure Actions:**
   - Click "Actions" tab → "New..."
   - Action: "Start a program"
   - Program/script: `dotnet`
   - Add arguments: `run --project "C:\path\to\TallySyncService\TallySyncService.csproj"`
   - Start in: `C:\path\to\TallySyncService`
   - Click OK

5. **Configure Settings:**
   - Click "Settings" tab
   - Check "Allow task to be run on demand"
   - Check "Run task as soon as possible after a scheduled start is missed"
   - Check "If the task fails, restart every: 1 minute"
   - Attempt to restart up to: 3 times
   - Click OK

### Managing the Windows Task

Using PowerShell:

```powershell
# View task status
Get-ScheduledTask -TaskName "TallySyncService"

# Start the task
Start-ScheduledTask -TaskName "TallySyncService"

# Stop the task
Stop-ScheduledTask -TaskName "TallySyncService"

# Disable the task
Disable-ScheduledTask -TaskName "TallySyncService"

# Enable the task
Enable-ScheduledTask -TaskName "TallySyncService"

# Remove the task
Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName "TallySyncService" -Confirm:$false
```

Using Task Scheduler GUI:
- Open Task Scheduler (`taskschd.msc`)
- Navigate to "Task Scheduler Library"
- Find "TallySyncService"
- Right-click for options (Run, End, Disable, Delete, etc.)

---

## Troubleshooting

### Linux

**Service not starting:**
```bash
# Check service status
systemctl --user status tallysync-user.service

# View detailed logs
journalctl --user -u tallysync-user.service -n 50

# Check if .NET is installed
dotnet --version
```

**Service fails after reboot:**
```bash
# Enable lingering (allows user services to run without login)
loginctl enable-linger $USER
```

### Windows

**Task not running:**
1. Open Task Scheduler
2. Right-click on "TallySyncService" → "Properties"
3. Check the "History" tab for errors
4. Verify the path to `dotnet` and the project file are correct

**Permission issues:**
- Make sure you ran the setup script as Administrator
- Verify that your user has permission to run .NET applications

### Common Issues (Both Platforms)

**Config not found:**
- Make sure `config.json` exists in the project directory
- Run `dotnet run -- --setup` to create it

**Not logged in:**
- Run `dotnet run -- --login <backend-url>` to authenticate

**Tally not starting:**
- Verify the Tally path in `config.json` is correct
- On Linux, make sure Wine is installed: `wine --version`

---

## Logs

### Linux
- Output: `~/.local/share/tallysync/output.log`
- Errors: `~/.local/share/tallysync/error.log`
- System logs: `journalctl --user -u tallysync-user.service`

### Windows
- Logs: `%LOCALAPPDATA%\TallySyncService\Logs\`
- Task history: Task Scheduler → TallySyncService → History tab

---

## Uninstalling Auto-Start

### Linux
```bash
systemctl --user stop tallysync-user.service
systemctl --user disable tallysync-user.service
rm ~/.config/systemd/user/tallysync-user.service
systemctl --user daemon-reload
```

### Windows
```powershell
Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName "TallySyncService" -Confirm:$false
```

Or use Task Scheduler GUI to delete the task.

#!/bin/bash

# TallySyncService - Linux Auto-Start Setup Script
# This script sets up the service to auto-start on user login using systemd user service

set -e

echo "╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════╗"
echo "║   TallySyncService - Linux Auto-Start Setup  ║"
echo "╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════╝"
echo ""

# Get the absolute path to the project directory
PROJECT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
SERVICE_FILE="tallysync-user.service"
SERVICE_NAME="tallysync-user.service"

# Create log directory
LOG_DIR="$HOME/.local/share/tallysync"
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
echo "✓ Created log directory: $LOG_DIR"

# Create systemd user directory if it doesn't exist
SYSTEMD_USER_DIR="$HOME/.config/systemd/user"
mkdir -p "$SYSTEMD_USER_DIR"
echo "✓ Created systemd user directory: $SYSTEMD_USER_DIR"

# Create a temporary service file with correct paths
TEMP_SERVICE="/tmp/tallysync-user.service"
cat > "$TEMP_SERVICE" << EOF
[Unit]
Description=Tally Sync Service - Auto sync Tally data to backend
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
WorkingDirectory=$PROJECT_DIR
ExecStart=/usr/bin/dotnet run --project $PROJECT_DIR/TallySyncService.csproj
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=30
StandardOutput=append:$LOG_DIR/output.log
StandardError=append:$LOG_DIR/error.log

# Environment variables (if needed)
Environment="DOTNET_CLI_TELEMETRY_OPTOUT=1"

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
EOF

# Copy service file to systemd user directory
cp "$TEMP_SERVICE" "$SYSTEMD_USER_DIR/$SERVICE_NAME"
echo "✓ Installed service file to: $SYSTEMD_USER_DIR/$SERVICE_NAME"

# Reload systemd user daemon
systemctl --user daemon-reload
echo "✓ Reloaded systemd user daemon"

# Enable the service to start on login
systemctl --user enable "$SERVICE_NAME"
echo "✓ Enabled service to start on user login"

# Start the service now
systemctl --user start "$SERVICE_NAME"
echo "✓ Started service"

echo ""
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo "✅ Auto-start setup complete!"
echo "═══════════════════════════════════════════════"
echo ""
echo "Service Commands:"
echo "  • Check status:  systemctl --user status $SERVICE_NAME"
echo "  • View logs:     journalctl --user -u $SERVICE_NAME -f"
echo "  • Stop service:  systemctl --user stop $SERVICE_NAME"
echo "  • Restart:       systemctl --user restart $SERVICE_NAME"
echo "  • Disable:       systemctl --user disable $SERVICE_NAME"
echo ""
echo "Log files:"
echo "  • Output: $LOG_DIR/output.log"
echo "  • Errors: $LOG_DIR/error.log"
echo ""
